zh.lifeSummary: Mrs. Sarah De Bois於1325年出生在英國肯特古德內斯同。她於1340年與John de Bois在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1390年在英國肯特多福享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs. Sarah De Bois于1325年出生在英国肯特古德內斯同。她于1340年与John de Bois在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1390年在英国肯特多福享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Dubois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Pierre, Fernand, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Michel, Alain, Luc.

French, Walloon, and English (of French Huguenot origin): topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ), or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Le Bois. This is the most frequent surname in Wallonia. It is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Haiti. In both England and North America it is sometimes translated into English as Wood . Compare Deboe , Debois , Deboy , Duboise , and Dubose .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Bois) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the several Huguenot ancestors bearing this name was Chrétien Du Bois from Flanders, whose son, Louis Du Bois (born 1626 in Lille, France, died 1696 in Kingston, NY), was among the Huguenots who founded the town of New Paltz, NY 1675–78.

variants.name: Boisvert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Donat, Lucien, Jacques, Gaston, Normand, Pierre, Gilles, Herve, Adrien.
French:
topographic name for someone who lived in a dense forest or perhaps a copse of evergreens, from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + vert, verd ‘green’.

variants.name: Boisclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Marcel, Aldege, Alphonse, Arsene, Edmour, Emile, Leopaul, Prudent, Rejean, Remi, Yves.

French: topographic name from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + clair ‘clear, light’, or a habitational name from (Le) Bois Clair, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Boies
meanings: Altered form of French   Bois  .

variants.name: Baskerville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Debois
meanings: Altered form of French   Dubois  , a cognate of 1 above.
French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ).

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Sarah De Bois was born in 1325, in Goodnestone, Kent, England. She married John de Bois in 1340, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1390, in Dover, Kent, England, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs. Sarah De Bois est née en 1325, à Goodnestone, Kent, Angleterre. Elle a épousé John de Bois en 1340, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1390, à Dover, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs. Sarah De Bois wurde 1325 in Goodnestone, Kent, England geboren. Sie hat 1340 in England John de Bois geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1390 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Dover, Kent, England.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs. Sarah De Bois è nata il nel 1325 nel Goodnestone, Kent, England. Lei sposato John de Bois nel 1340, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1390, nel Dover, Kent, England, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.Goodnestone, Kent, England: イギリスケントグドネストーン
ja.Dover, Kent, England: イギリスケントドバー
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs. Sarah De Boisは1325年にイギリスケントグドネストーンで生まれた。1340年にイギリスでJohn de Boisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1390年にイギリスケントドバーで65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Sarah De Bois(은)는 1325년 영국 켄트 구드네스톤에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1340년 영국에서 John de Bois(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1390년 영국 켄트 도버에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs. Sarah De Bois nasceu em 1325, em Goodnestone, Kent, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com John de Bois em 1340, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1390, em Dover, Kent, Inglaterra, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs. Sarah De Bois родилась в 1325 г. в местности гуднестоун, кэнт, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Bois в 1340 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1390 г. в местности довир, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs. Sarah De Bois nació en 1325, en Goodnestone, Kent, Inglaterra. Se casó con John de Bois en 1340, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1390, en Dover, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 65 años.
